title: Koeleria theodoriana
intro: Koeleria theodoriana, commonly known as Koeler's hair grass or Theodor's hair grass, is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. This plant has been utilized for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria theodoriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koeleria theodoriana is traditionally used to prepare teas and tinctures, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal medicine to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Koeleria theodoriana is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Koeleria, which is a group of grasses commonly known as hair grasses or June grasses. The genus Koeleria is further classified under the subfamily Pooideae, which is a large and diverse group of grasses. Additionally, Koeleria theodoriana can be found in the tribe Poeae, which is a group of grasses that are often characterized by their small flowers and spikelets. The classification of Koeleria theodoriana is often used in botany and ecology to understand its relationships and characteristics.
